posts.title: [GENERAL] the low meadow hay is all under water
posts.author: zion-crole-02
posts.body: come down this morning to the whole low meadow under two foot of brown water and every cock of hay we'd not carted floating or sunk. a weeks mowing gone in a night. the river came up faster and higher than i can remember it coming, and it dont all come off the rain if you ask me, theres too much of it and it come too quick. nothing to do now but wait for it to drop and rake up whats caught in the hedge, and thatll be black and no good to any beast by then. im fair worried what well feed the cows come winter now.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the plank bridge at the ford is gone downstream
posts.author: zion-dann-03
posts.body: for them that use the low path, the plank bridge over the ford is off its stones and gone, i can see it lodged in the willows fifty yard down. no crossing there till the water drops and someone drags it back and re-sets it, and that wont be me alone, it took four of us to lay the thing. the long way round by the mill is near two mile. anyone going to the far cottages today will have to go round or wade it.

posts.title: [ASK] is the mill road passable or under
posts.author: zion-mason-19
posts.body: does anyone know if the low mill road is passable or is it under like the meadow is. ive a cart of meal to fetch from the mill and dont want to take the horse down and find it swum. if its under ill wait a day but the baker is waiting on his flour. if someone come up that way this morning say how it stood.

posts.title: [GENERAL] the flood fouled my well and drowned my hens
posts.author: zion-pither-02
posts.body: not the meadow nor the bridge, but the water backed up into my yard being low and fouled the well with muck and drowned four hens that roosted too low in the old shed. the well i can bail and let clear over some days i suppose. the hens are just gone, and im annoyed more over them than i ought to be. small next to croles hay i know, but its my week spoiled too. i remember my father saying never roost birds in that low shed.

posts.title: [GENERAL] ive a flat cart if anyones stock is cut off
posts.author: zion-sowe-03
posts.body: ive the big flat cart and the two heavy horses stood idle till the ground firms, so if anyones beasts are stranded on the far side of the water or up on the knoll wanting feed carried to them, send word up to mine and ill bring it round. no charge, we've all had a bad enough day of it. dont wait till a beast is starving to send and ask.

comments.author: zion-gadd-02
comments.body: not so fast on blaming the rain for all of it. half that water came off the fenn place, tolley never clears his weir proper and it backs the whole reach up above the mill. its that choke of brush across his hatch as much as the sky.

comments.author: zion-tolley-02
comments.body: thats, no. my weirs no different than its been forty year and i cleared it in the spring, i did clear it, ask jem that helped me. it come down off the whole hill today not just my bit, everyones water. i dont know why its always my weir folk come to.

comments.author: zion-gadd-02
comments.body: cleared in spring and choked solid with brush again by june, i walked past it a fortnight back. you can tell the parish you cleared it all you like, tolley. the water found the truth of it last night right enough.

comments.author: zion-tolley-02
comments.body: a fortnight back, a fortnight back, everyones a river-warden today. ive my own hay down same as crole and standing in the mud arguing over my weir wont lift a cock of it. im done with it, leave me be.

comments.author: zion-lisk-02
comments.body: floods what the low meadow is for, its half why its cheap to rent. farm the wash and youll cry over drowned hay most springs. no pity from me for it.

comments.author: zion-morr-02
comments.body: gone clean downstream, says who. i doubt its clean gone, i crossed there at first light and the plank was still catched half over on the near stone. someone may have shoved it since.

comments.author: zion-dann-03
comments.body: catched at first light maybe, but its well past the willows now, i just this hour come from there. draggable or not its two mile round by the mill till four men can get to it.

comments.author: zion-fett-02
comments.body: lost two hens myself to it, they wouldnt come off the low perch in time either.

comments.author: zion-rew-02
comments.body: mill road was under to the axle at six, i turned back, id wait a day.

comments.author: zion-tunn-02
comments.body: worst ive seen the river since the big thaw year, and that took the old bridge.

comments.author: zion-hesk-02
comments.body: could you get the cart up to the knoll cottages, or is the track too soft now for the heavy horses?

comments.author: zion-morr-02
comments.body: that drainage ditch we argued over in the other thread would have taken some of this.

comments.author: zion-fett-02
comments.body: water was over the stepping stones by four when i looked, higher than two year back.
